concept.title: How DITA-OT Is Built and Its Core Architecture
concept.shortdesc: DITA-OT implements a multi-stage, map-driven architecture where preprocessing dominates the complexity and the temp directory is the single source of truth for debugging.

section.title: The mental model
section.p: DITA-OT implements a multi-stage, map-driven architecture. Each stage examines some or all of the content; some stages generate temporary files used later, while others generate final outputs. Most processing happens in a temporary working directory, and source files are never modified.
You can think of every transtype as:
section.codeblock: inputs (map/topics) | | preprocessing (common) v normalized / resolved intermediates | | format-specific processing (HTML5, PDF, etc.) v output
section.p: This is the key to fast debugging: if the intermediate artifacts are wrong, downstream output will be wrong.

section.title: Stages, temp dir, and why preprocessing dominates

p.b: Stages are designed around responsibility boundaries

ul.li: map-first or mixed preprocessing (depending on pipeline)
transformation steps for topics and maps

section.p: The "hard" part is not HTML or PDF formatting—it's the DITA model itself: keys, scoping, references, reuse, filtering, chunking.

p.b: The temp directory is your truth source

section.p: Because DITA-OT does not modify sources and performs most work in a temp dir, you can debug by comparing:

ul.li: chunked result lists (how the build decided to split output)

section.p: DITA-OT explicitly describes this temp-dir-based processing behavior.
